title: RD-4 grenade launcher

The '''RD-4 grenade launcher''',<ref name="BF1 guide" /> also known as the '''radiation launcher''',<ref name="BF2">''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''</ref> was a type of [[Grenade launcher/Legends|grenade launcher]] which fired [[radiation grenade]]s that was manufactured by [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc.]]. The launcher could configure the fuse of the [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]], so that they either exploded on impact or could be bounced off surfaces to detonate after a few seconds.<ref name="BF1 guide" /><ref name="BF2 guide">[[Star Wars Battlefront II: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Battlefront II'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> Early in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the launcher was used by [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[OOM pilot battle droid/Legends|OOM pilot battle droids]] on various battlefields, such as the [[First Battle of Kamino]] and [[Battle of Rhen Var (Clone Wars)|Battle of Rhen Var]].<ref name="BF1" /> Late in the war, it was also adopted by the [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuards]].<ref name="BF2" />

==Behind the scenes==
The RD-4 grenade launcher serves as the unique primary weapon for the pilot droid in ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]''. In ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'', the pilot class was rebranded as the engineer class, and in place of a unique primary faction for each faction's pilot unit, the engineers' primary weapon was standardized to a shotgun. As a result, the radiation launcher was passed onto the MagnaGuards, a special unit, as a secondary weapon.

Like all grenade launchers in the series, the weapon's model is based on the [[T-21 light repeating blaster/Legends|T-21 light repeating blaster]].
